# The netboot pipeline: build → serve → boot 🟡

**Goal.** Turn a root filesystem into something a machine boots over the network, at three fidelity tiers — minimal, networked-busybox, full-systemd.

**Audience:** you can build a chroot and run a VM  
**Effort:** ⏱ half-day+  
**Prerequisites:** [Containers from first principles](path-container-internals.md)

| # | Lab | You'll learn | Checkpoint (observable) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | [`chroot-netboot-minimal.toml`](../chroot-netboot-minimal.toml) 🔑 | kernel + BusyBox + an auto-written `/init`, no networking — Tier 1. | boots to a BusyBox shell entirely in RAM. |
| 2 | [`chroot-netboot-busybox.toml`](../chroot-netboot-busybox.toml) 🔑 | add iproute2/ping/curl → a fast, networked RAM shell — Tier 2. | (paired with the next step) `curl` succeeds from a RAM-only shell. |
| 3 | [`vm-netboot-direct.toml`](../vm-netboot-direct.toml) | boot the Tier-2 initrd via QEMU `-kernel/-initrd` — the short debug loop, no iPXE. | kernel + initrd boot directly to the networked shell. |
| 4 | [`chroot-netboot-full.toml`](../chroot-netboot-full.toml) 🔑 | systemd PID 1 + SSH + cloud-init from RAM (~300–500 MB initrd) — Tier 3. | (with the next step) you SSH into a diskless systemd system. |
| 5 | [`vm-netboot-full.toml`](../vm-netboot-full.toml) | give the VM enough RAM to unpack ~1 GB of initrd. | boots the full systemd initrd; `systemctl` works with no disk. |
| 6 | [`debian-http-boot`](../debian-http-boot/) 🔗 | Finnegan's boot-over-HTTP: a full systemd rootfs run from RAM via a verbatim hand-rolled `/init`. | explain why initramfs-as-rootfs needs no `switch_root`; MANUAL_TESTING boots it end-to-end. |
| 7 | [`podman-netboot-server.toml`](../podman-netboot-server.toml) 🪶 | serve the kernel + initrd over HTTP (:8181), rootless. | `curl http://localhost:8181/…` fetches the artifacts. |
| 8 | [`vm-netboot-ipxe.toml`](../vm-netboot-ipxe.toml) | an iPXE disk fetches kernel + initrd over HTTP — simulates real PXE hardware. | iPXE chainloads and boots the served initrd. |
| 9 | [`pxe-boot-mechanics`](../pxe-boot-mechanics/) | watch the transport (TFTP then HTTP), then UEFI Secure Boot enforcement. | `pxe-fetch.sh` records a TFTP then an HTTP fetch; Secure Boot rejects an unsigned iPXE binary. |

### Optional side-quests

- [`docker-examples/docker-netboot-server.toml`](../docker-examples/docker-netboot-server.toml) — the rootful Docker equivalent of the serve step.
- [`podman-pxe-dhcp.toml`](../podman-pxe-dhcp.toml) — 🔑 real-hardware ProxyDHCP + TFTP responder (needs `--network=host` to hear DHCP broadcasts).
